questions.question: What component is responsible for temporarily holding data for quick access by the CPU?
Which tool is best for testing the voltage output of a power supply?
What type of expansion slot is commonly used for modern graphics cards?
What does BIOS stand for?
Which type of cable is used for high-speed network connections and has four twisted pairs?
What is the main purpose of thermal paste?
choices: Conduct heat between CPU and heatsink
questions.correct: Conduct heat between CPU and heatsink

questions.question: Which mobile connection type is best suited for high-speed data and global roaming?
What is the first step in the CompTIA troubleshooting methodology?
Which file system is used by default in Windows 10?
What command is used to check the current IP configuration in Windows?
Commonly used data types do NOT include:
What is a hypervisor in virtualization?
choices: A tool to manage hardware components
A software that allows multiple operating systems to run on a single physical machine
questions.correct: A software that allows multiple operating systems to run on a single physical machine

questions.question: What type of memory is volatile and loses data when power is turned off?
Which of the following is the default file system for Windows 10?
True or False? Authentication is the process of verifying the identity of a user.
The four basic file attributes, regardless of the operating system, are:
Which of the following ports is commonly used for connecting a monitor?
Which protocol is used to send email from a client to a server?
Which of these provides resources such as virtual storage, physical machines, or virtual machines?
The index on the file system, which points to where a file is located on a hard drive, is called the?
What is the first step in the troubleshooting methodology?
choices: Establish a theory of probable cause

questions.question: Which type of malware tricks a user into running a program that does something different than expected?
Which of the following servers is responsible for providing IP addresses to clients on the network?
Servers provide a number of services to a network. The server used to deliver Internet sites is the:
What is the function of a GPU in a computer?
What does CSS mean?
Which connector is commonly used for internal hard drives?
If you wanted to remove personal information from a computer in order to sell it what would you do?
Fans are important to manage internal airflow for computers. The air is warmest by which of the following components?
A technician is troubleshooting what appears to be a RAM issue on a PC. Which of the following symptoms would indicate if this is a RAM issue?
Hard drive cases use which size of standard screw thread and length?
If you wished to encrypt communications from an employee's computer back to the office network, which would you use?
Which command is used to list files in a directory in Linux?
Power-on-self-test (POST) beeps may also include an error code. Which error code indicates a hard drive error?
How can you add a comment in a JavaScript (.js) file?
Which of the following is the correct syntax to redirect a url using JavaScript?
A central location for a Linux distribution's officially supported software packages is called a:
At which OSI layer does a router primarily operate?
Which OSI layer is responsible for end-to-end error recovery and flow control?
A host has the IP address 192.168.10.45/27. What is the network address of its subnet?
How many usable host addresses exist in a /26 subnet?
Which subnet mask is represented by /21?
Which command correctly assigns a switch port to VLAN 30?
What is the purpose of a trunk port on a switch?
choices: To carry traffic for a single VLAN only
To carry traffic for multiple VLANs between switches
To assign a port to the native VLAN
questions.correct: To carry traffic for multiple VLANs between switches

questions.question: Which STP port state forwards data frames AND learns MAC addresses?
Which bridge is elected as the root bridge in STP?
choices: The bridge with the highest MAC address
The bridge with the lowest bridge priority, then lowest MAC address
The bridge with the most ports
The bridge with the highest bridge priority
questions.correct: The bridge with the lowest bridge priority, then lowest MAC address

questions.question: What is the default administrative distance of OSPF?
Which routing protocol uses Dijkstra's Shortest Path First (SPF) algorithm?
What is the maximum hop count for RIP version 2?
A TCP segment has both the SYN and ACK flags set. What stage of the 3-way handshake does this represent?
choices: Step 2 – Server acknowledges and sends its own SYN
questions.correct: Step 2 – Server acknowledges and sends its own SYN

questions.question: Which protocol provides connectionless, best-effort delivery at the transport layer?
Which type of NAT maps multiple inside local addresses to a single public IP address using port numbers?
Where should a standard ACL be placed for optimal filtering efficiency?
choices: As close to the source as possible
As close to the destination as possible
questions.correct: As close to the destination as possible

questions.question: What is the correct order of messages in the DHCP lease process?
Which IPv6 address type is the equivalent of RFC 1918 private addresses in IPv4?
Which Cisco switch security feature prevents rogue DHCP servers by restricting DHCP replies to trusted ports only?
Which 802.11 standard introduced MIMO and operates on both 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz bands?
What type of malware disguises itself as legitimate software to trick users into installing it?
Which type of malware is capable of self-replicating and spreading across a network WITHOUT needing to attach itself to a host file?
What is the primary purpose of a rootkit?
choices: To encrypt files and demand a ransom
To gain and maintain privileged access while hiding its presence
To log keystrokes and steal credentials
questions.correct: To gain and maintain privileged access while hiding its presence

questions.question: Which of the following BEST describes a computer virus?
choices: Malware that spreads without user interaction
Malware that attaches itself to a host file and spreads when that file is executed
Malware that secretly records user activity
Malware that locks the user out of their system
questions.correct: Malware that attaches itself to a host file and spreads when that file is executed

questions.question: What distinguishes ransomware from other types of malware?
choices: It hides deep in the operating system kernel
It replicates itself across network shares
It encrypts a victim's data and demands payment for the decryption key
It silently monitors user behavior without causing damage
questions.correct: It encrypts a victim's data and demands payment for the decryption key

questions.question: A piece of malware is silently capturing everything a user types, including passwords and credit card numbers. What type of malware is this?
Which type of rootkit is the most difficult to detect and remove because it infects the Master Boot Record (MBR)?
What is a botnet?
choices: A network of firewalls protecting a system
A collection of infected machines controlled remotely by an attacker
A secure tunnel between two networks
questions.correct: A collection of infected machines controlled remotely by an attacker

questions.question: Spyware and adware are both types of malware. What is the key difference between them?
choices: Adware encrypts files; spyware does not
Spyware collects user data secretly; adware primarily delivers unwanted advertisements
Adware spreads via networks; spyware requires physical access
Spyware is always more destructive than adware
questions.correct: Spyware collects user data secretly; adware primarily delivers unwanted advertisements

questions.question: Which of the following is a defining characteristic of a worm compared to a virus?
choices: A worm requires a host file to spread
A worm can only infect Windows systems
A worm spreads independently without needing to attach to another file
A worm always demands a ransom from the victim
questions.correct: A worm spreads independently without needing to attach to another file

questions.question: What type of malware creates a hidden backdoor on an infected system, allowing an attacker to return at any time?
A logic bomb is best described as:
choices: Malware that spreads through email attachments
Malware that activates only when a specific condition or event is triggered
A type of rootkit targeting the BIOS
A virus that corrupts executable files
questions.correct: Malware that activates only when a specific condition or event is triggered

questions.question: Which term describes legitimate-looking software that secretly performs malicious actions in the background?
What makes a polymorphic virus particularly difficult to detect with traditional antivirus software?
choices: It changes its code or signature each time it replicates
It hides in the system BIOS
It requires no host file to spread
questions.correct: It changes its code or signature each time it replicates

questions.question: A kernel-mode rootkit is more dangerous than a user-mode rootkit because:
choices: It operates with the highest level of system privileges, making it harder to detect
It is delivered only via phishing emails
questions.correct: It operates with the highest level of system privileges, making it harder to detect

questions.question: Which type of malware is specifically designed to mine cryptocurrency on a victim's machine without their knowledge?
What is the term for malware that records and transmits a user's web browsing habits and personal information to a third party?
Which of the following BEST describes the difference between a virus and a rootkit?
choices: A virus hides itself from the OS; a rootkit replicates by attaching to files
A virus replicates by attaching to files; a rootkit hides malicious activity and maintains privileged access
A rootkit always encrypts data; a virus does not
There is no difference — they are the same type of malware
questions.correct: A virus replicates by attaching to files; a rootkit hides malicious activity and maintains privileged access

questions.question: Which of the following is the MOST effective first step when responding to a ransomware infection?
choices: Reformat all drives on the network
Isolate the infected system from the network to prevent further spread
questions.correct: Isolate the infected system from the network to prevent further spread

questions.question: A fileless malware attack is dangerous primarily because:
choices: It always arrives via USB drives
It resides in memory and leaves little to no trace on the hard drive, evading traditional file-based detection
It can only be stopped by reinstalling the OS
It exclusively targets mobile operating systems
questions.correct: It resides in memory and leaves little to no trace on the hard drive, evading traditional file-based detection
